sections.dtia_summary: Allows disclosures for directory services and emergency calls under specific conditions, relevant for cross-border data transfers involving emergency services and directory information.

requirements.text: Disclosures for directory assistance services must be made by or on behalf of a carriage service provider
Disclosures for public number directories must be made by or on behalf of a carriage service provider
Disclosures for research must be specified in an instrument under subsection (3)
Recipient must hold authorization under the integrated public number database scheme for disclosures related to directory services
Emergency call disclosures are exempt from privacy prohibitions if information relates to emergency service numbers and it is unreasonable to obtain consent

sections.source_quote: 292 (c) the disclosure is made to another person for purposes connected with: (i) the provision of directory assistance services by or on behalf of a carriage service provider; (ii) the publication and maintenance of a public number directory; (iv) the conduct of research of a kind specified in an instrument under subsection (3); and (d) if the disclosure to the other person is for a purpose covered by subparagraph (c)(ii) or (iv)—the other person holds an authorisation in force under the integrated public number database scheme permitting the other person to use and disclose the information or document.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Emergency threat disclosures exempt from privacy prohibitions
sections.summary: This section exempts disclosures made by a person to prevent or lessen a serious threat to life or health if it is unreasonable to obtain consent from the affected person. The exemption applies when the person believes on reasonable grounds that the disclosure is necessary to address the threat.
sections.dtia_summary: Exempts emergency threat disclosures from privacy prohibitions when it is unreasonable to obtain consent, relevant for cross-border data transfers involving life-threatening situations.

requirements.text: Disclosures are exempt from privacy prohibitions if the person believes on reasonable grounds that the disclosure is reasonably necessary to prevent or lessen a serious threat to life or health
It must be unreasonable or impracticable to obtain the other person's consent to the disclosure or use

sections.source_quote: 287 Division 2 does not prohibit a disclosure or use by a person (the first person) of information or a document if: (a) the information or document relates to the affairs or personal particulars (including any unlisted telephone number or any address) of another person; and (ab) it is unreasonable or impracticable to obtain the other person's consent to the disclosure or use; and (b) the first person believes on reasonable grounds that the disclosure or use is reasonably necessary to prevent or lessen a serious threat to the life or health of a person.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Implicit consent for communications between parties
sections.summary: This section exempts disclosures made by a person if the sender and recipient of a communication would reasonably have consented to the disclosure or use, given the circumstances. The exemption applies when it is reasonable to expect consent based on the nature of the communication.
sections.dtia_summary: Exempts communications where implicit consent is reasonably expected, relevant for cross-border data transfers involving private communications.

requirements.text: Disclosures are exempt from privacy prohibitions if the sender and recipient would have consented to the disclosure or use if they had been aware of the disclosure or use

sections.source_quote: 290 Section 276 does not prohibit a disclosure or use by a person if: (a) the information or document relates to the contents or substance of a communication made by another person; and (b) having regard to all the relevant circumstances, it might reasonably be expected that the sender and the recipient of the communication would have consented to the disclosure or use, if they had been aware of the disclosure or use.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Carriage service provider disclosures for customers
sections.summary: This section permits disclosures by carriage service providers to other carriers or service providers for business purposes, including supplying services to customers or former customers. The exemption applies when the information relates to a customer or former customer and the disclosure is for service provision or related activities.
sections.dtia_summary: Allows disclosures between service providers for customer-related services, relevant for cross-border data transfers involving telecommunications services.

requirements.text: Disclosures by carriage service providers to other carriers or service providers are exempt from privacy prohibitions for business purposes
The information must relate to a customer or former customer of the service provider
The disclosure must be for the supply or proposed supply of carriage services or content services

sections.source_quote: 291 (1) Section 276 does not prohibit a disclosure or use by a person of (a) the disclosure or use is made by or on behalf of: (i) a carrier (the first carrier); or (ii) a carriage service provider (the first provider); and with, any other carrier or service provider carrying on its business as such a carrier or provider; and (c) the information or document relates to a person (the third person) who is a customer or former customer of: (i) the first carrier or the first provider; or (ii) the other carrier or the other provider; and with: (i) the supply, or proposed supply, by the other carrier or other provider to the third person of a carriage service or a content service; or (ii) the supply, or proposed supply, by the other carrier or other provider to the third person of goods or services for use in connection with the supply of a carriage service or a content service.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Exceptions for threats to life or health
sections.summary: Disclosures related to another person's affairs or personal particulars may be used without consent if it is unreasonable to obtain consent, or if the disclosure is necessary to prevent or lessen a serious threat to life or health. The person must believe on reasonable grounds that the disclosure is necessary for such purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: Allows data sharing in emergencies where threats to life or health are involved, with specific conditions on necessity and belief.

requirements.text: Must not disclose or use information unless it is unreasonable or impracticable to obtain the other person's consent
Must be for the purpose of preventing or lessening a serious threat to life or health
Believes on reasonable grounds that disclosure is reasonably necessary to prevent or lessen a serious threat to life or health

sections.source_quote: 300 (b) the information or the contents of the document relate to the affairs or personal particulars (including any unlisted telephone number or any address) of another person. (2) The first person must not disclose or use the information or document unless: (a) it is unreasonable or impracticable to obtain the other person's consent to the disclosure or use; and (b) either of the following apply: (i) the disclosure or use is for the purpose of, or in connection with, preventing or lessening a serious threat to the life or health of a person; (ii) the first person believes on reasonable grounds that the disclosure or use is reasonably necessary to prevent or lessen a serious threat to the life or health of a person.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Conditions for disclosure of integrated public number database information
sections.summary: This section specifies conditions under which disclosures of information from an integrated public number database are permitted to carriers or carriage service providers. It requires that the information does not relate to unlisted telephone numbers, the disclosure is for supply or proposed supply of location-dependent carriage services, and the disclosure is connected to such services. The section also states that sections 276 and 277 do not prohibit such disclosures when these conditions are met.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines specific conditions that must be met for cross-border transfers of integrated public number database information to carriers, ensuring disclosures are only for location-dependent carriage services and not involving unlisted numbers.

requirements.text: The information must not relate to unlisted telephone numbers
The disclosure must be to a carrier or carriage service provider
The disclosure must be for supply or proposed supply of location-dependent carriage services

sections.source_quote: 301 (a) the information or document relates to information (other than information relating to an unlisted telephone number) contained in an integrated public number database; and (b) the disclosure is to a carrier or a carriage service provider; and (c) the disclosure is made for a purpose of, or is connected with, the supply, or proposed supply, by a person of a location dependent carriage service.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: ACMA scheme for integrated public number database authorisations
sections.summary: This section outlines the requirements for the ACMA to create a scheme for granting authorisations related to integrated public number databases. The scheme must cover applications, assessments, duration of authorisations, and notification procedures. Applicants must specify the purpose of authorisations, which must relate to public number directories or research.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes the framework for authorising cross-border transfers of integrated public number database information, requiring specific purposes and notification mechanisms to ensure compliance with data transfer conditions.

requirements.text: The scheme must provide for making applications for authorisations
The scheme must provide for assessment of applications
The scheme must specify the period for which authorisations are in force
The scheme must require notification of decisions to the person maintaining the integrated public number database
Applicants must specify the purpose for which authorisations are sought

sections.source_quote: 295B (1) The scheme must make provision for and in relation to the following matters: (a) the making of applications for authorisations; (b) the assessment of applications; (c) the period for which authorisations are to be in force; (d) the notification of decisions under the scheme (including to the person who maintains the integrated public number database referred to in paragraph 285(1A)(a)). (2) The scheme must require an applicant for an authorisation to specify the purpose for which the authorisation is sought.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: ACMA administrative powers under integrated public number database scheme
sections.summary: This section allows the ACMA to grant administrative powers to the ACMA through the scheme, enabling decisions on matters related to the integrated public number database scheme. The scheme may include ancillary provisions and is not limited by other sections, allowing for variations without revocation.
sections.dtia_summary: This section permits the ACMA to delegate administrative powers under the integrated public number database scheme, facilitating adjustments and operational flexibility without revocation.

requirements.text: The scheme may empower the ACMA to make administrative decisions
The scheme may contain ancillary or incidental provisions as the ACMA considers appropriate

sections.source_quote: 295H The scheme may make provision for or in relation to a particular matter by empowering the ACMA to make decisions of an administrative character. 295J The scheme may contain such ancillary or incidental provisions as the ACMA considers appropriate.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Compliance with integrated public number database scheme authorisations
sections.summary: This section mandates that persons must not contravene directions under the scheme and specifies that such directions are civil penalty provisions. It also states that directions given under the scheme are not legislative instruments.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures compliance with scheme authorisations by prohibiting contraventions and clarifying that directions are civil penalties, not legislative instruments.

requirements.text: A person must not contravene a direction under subsection (2)
Subsection (3) is a civil penalty provision
A direction given under subsection (2) is not a legislative instrument

sections.source_quote: 309 (3) A person must not contravene a direction under subsection (2). (4) Subsection (3) is a civil penalty provision. Note: provisions.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Emergency disclosure of integrated public number database information
sections.summary: This section outlines the conditions under which emergency management persons may disclose integrated public number database information, including restrictions on use for purposes other than alerting to emergencies. It specifies that such disclosures must not be for non-emergency purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines when emergency disclosures of integrated public number database information are permitted, ensuring they are only for emergency purposes and not for other uses.

requirements.text: The use or disclosure of information must not be for a purpose connected with persons being alerted to an emergency or a likely emergency

sections.source_quote: 313 (b) the person uses or discloses the information; and (c) the use or disclosure referred to in paragraph (b) of this subsection is not for a purpose connected with persons being alerted to an emergency or a likely emergency.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Reporting requirements for emergency disclosures
sections.summary: This section requires emergency management persons to provide written reports to the AFP Minister and ACMA after disclosing integrated public number database information during emergencies, detailing the emergency, number of telephone numbers disclosed, and recipients of disclosures.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates detailed reporting for emergency disclosures of integrated public number database information, ensuring transparency and accountability in emergency response scenarios.

requirements.text: Emergency management persons must provide written reports to AFP Minister and ACMA within 2 months after financial year end
Reports must include description of emergency or likely emergency and location
Reports must include number of telephone numbers disclosed and day of disclosure
Reports must include number of persons to whom numbers were disclosed and purpose of each disclosure

sections.source_quote: 295ZB (1) If an emergency management person discloses relevant information, the person must give a written report to the AFP Minister and to the ACMA that covers the following matters: (a) if the disclosure occurred under subsection 295V(1) or (2)—a description of the emergency or likely emergency concerned and its location; (b) in any case—the number of telephone numbers that were disclosed and the day that disclosure occurred; (c) in any case—the number of persons to whom the emergency management person disclosed those numbers and the purpose of each disclosure.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Restrictions on disclosure of information under authorisation
sections.summary: A person holding an authorisation under the integrated public number database scheme must not disclose or use information except for the purpose covered by subsection 285(1A)(c)(iv). If no authorisation is held, the person must not disclose or use the information at all. This restriction applies to information disclosed for purposes permitted by subsection (2) or this subsection.
sections.dtia_summary: Restricts cross-border data sharing when authorisation is held; requires specific purpose limitation for data disclosed under the scheme.

requirements.text: Must not disclose or use information except for the purpose covered by subsection 285(1A)(c)(iv)
Must not disclose or use information if no authorisation is held

sections.source_quote: 317 (b) the disclosure is for a purpose covered by subparagraph 285(1A)(c)(iv); then: (c) during the period the person holds an authorisation in force under the integrated public number database scheme in relation to the information or document—the person must not disclose or use the information or document except for that purpose; and (d) if the person does not hold such an authorisation—the person must not disclose or use the information or document.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Restrictions on location-dependent carriage services
sections.summary: Information disclosed for location-dependent carriage services must not be used for any purpose other than the supply or proposed supply of such services. Location-dependent carriage services require information about end users' addresses to operate.
sections.dtia_summary: Restricts data sharing for location-dependent services, requiring specific purpose limitation for carriage services that depend on user addresses.

requirements.text: Must not disclose or use information except for the purpose of, or in connection with, the supply, or proposed supply, by a person of a location dependent carriage service

sections.source_quote: 319 (1) If information or a document is disclosed to a person as permitted by section 291A or this subsection, a person must not disclose or use the information or document except for the purpose of, or in connection with, the supply, or proposed supply, by a person of a location dependent carriage service.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Record-keeping for disclosures
sections.summary: Carriers, service providers, and number-database operators must retain records of disclosures for 3 years. Records must include the name of the discloser, disclosure date, grounds for disclosure, and specific details depending on the legal basis.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed record-keeping for data disclosures, critical for DTIA to track data flows and legal bases.

requirements.text: Must retain records of disclosures for 3 years
Must include name of person who disclosed information
Must include date of disclosure
Must include statement of grounds for disclosure

sections.source_quote: 305 (2) The carrier, carriage service provider or number-database operator must retain the notification for 3 years. 306 (2) If the person is a carrier, carriage service provider or number-database operator, the carrier, provider or operator must: (a) make a record of the disclosure as soon as practicable after the disclosure and, in any event, within 5 days after the disclosure; and (b) retain that record for 3 years.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Detailed record-keeping requirements
sections.summary: Records must specify the discloser's name, disclosure date, grounds for disclosure, and additional details such as legal basis, warrant information, or authorisation details depending on the circumstances. Records may be in written or electronic form.
sections.dtia_summary: Detailed record-keeping requirements for data disclosures, essential for DTIA to verify legal bases and data flows.

requirements.text: Must include name of person who disclosed information
Must include date of disclosure
Must include statement of grounds for disclosure
Must include details of legal basis for disclosure if applicable

sections.source_quote: 306 (5) A record made under subsection (2) or (3) must set out: (a) the name of the person who disclosed the information or document concerned; (b) the date of the disclosure; (c) a statement of the grounds for the disclosure; and (d) if the disclosure is made on the grounds of an authorisation under the Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979: (i) the name of the person who made the authorisation; (ii) the date of the making of the authorisation;

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Record-keeping for authorisations
sections.summary: Records must include specific details such as the name of the authorising body, date of authorisation, and information about the type of data disclosed, particularly for carriage services. Records may be in written or electronic form.
sections.dtia_summary: Detailed record-keeping for authorisations, critical for DTIA to track data flows and legal bases.

requirements.text: Must include name of person who made the authorisation
Must include date of making the authorisation
Must include details of the type of information disclosed

sections.source_quote: 306 (5) (d) if the disclosure is made on the grounds of an authorisation under the Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979: (i) the name of the person who made the authorisation; (ii) the date of the making of the authorisation; (e) if paragraph (d) does not apply and the disclosure was at the request of another body or person: (i) the name of the body or person; (ii) the date of the request;

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Record compilation requirements for disclosures
sections.summary: This section mandates specific details for records of disclosures made under authorisation. It requires the name of the disclosing person, disclosure dates (first and last), grounds for disclosure, and the authorising person with their authorisation date. Records may be kept in written or electronic form. Violation results in a fine up to 300 penalty units.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires precise documentation of disclosure details for compliance with data transfer mechanisms, critical for ensuring transparency in cross-border data sharing activities.

requirements.text: The name of the person or persons who made the disclosure or disclosures
The date of the disclosure (first and last) if multiple disclosures are made
A statement of the grounds for the disclosure or disclosures
The name of the person who made the authorisation and the date of the making of the authorisation
Records may be made, given or retained in written or electronic form

sections.source_quote: 325 (a) the name of the person or persons who made the disclosure or disclosures; and (b) one of the following: (i) if only 1 disclosure is made because of the authorisation—the date of the disclosure; (ii) if more than 1 disclosure is made because of the authorisation—the date of the first disclosure and the date of the last disclosure; and (c) a statement of the grounds for the disclosure or disclosures; and (d) the name of the person who made the authorisation and the date of the making of the authorisation.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Annual reporting on disclosures to ACMA
sections.summary: This section requires carriers, carriage service providers, and number-database operators to submit annual reports to ACMA within two months after the end of a financial year if disclosures were made under section 306 or 306A. The reports must include specific information about disclosures as required by ACMA, ensuring ongoing oversight of data handling practices.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates annual reporting on cross-border data disclosures, critical for DTIA compliance to ensure timely and accurate reporting of international data transfers.

requirements.text: Carriers, carriage service providers, or number-database operators must submit written reports to ACMA within 2 months after the end of a financial year
Reports must set out information about disclosures as required by ACMA

sections.source_quote: 326 (1) If: information or a document is disclosed during a financial year; and either: (i) under section 306 or 306A, a carrier, carriage service provider or number-database operator makes a record of the disclosure; or (ii) under section 306 or 306A, a carrier, carriage service provider or number-database operator is given a copy of a record of the disclosure; the carrier, carriage service provider or number-database operator must, within 2 months after the end of the financial year, give the ACMA a written report relating to the disclosure.
